     The Numbers Business Report

Walking Tall (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $46,213,824Details
International Box Office $1,642,559
Worldwide Box Office $47,856,383
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When decorated military officer Chris Vaughn returns to his hometown in the state of Washington to find work, he finds that the lumber mill has closed, and the town’s big business is now a seedy casino operated by one of his old schoolmates, Jay Hamilton. Aided by his loyal friend Ray Templeton, Vaughn tries to make a stand against the area’s crime and corruption, but, finding no help from the police, he must take matters into his own hands.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$15,501,114 (33.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.98 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:96.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$56,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,836 opening theaters/2,836 max. theaters, 5.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $89,004,406

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 2nd, 2004 (Wide) by MGM
International Releases: August 12th, 2004 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: September 28th, 2004 by MGM Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense violence, sexual content, drug material and language
Running Time: 86 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Returning Soldiers, Revenge, War
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Hyde Park Entertainment, Mandeville Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When decorated military officer Chris Vaughn returns to his hometown in the state of Washington to find work, he finds that the lumber mill has closed, and the town’s big business is now a seedy casino operated by one of his old schoolmates, Jay Hamilton. Aided by his loyal friend Ray Templeton, Vaughn tries to make a stand against the area’s crime and corruption, but, finding no help from the police, he must take matters into his own hands.
